title: Aathadi Enna Udambu Song Lyrics Sindhu Nathi Poo Movie
stanzas:
Aathadi Enna Udambu
Adi anganga pachcha narambu
iymponnaal senja odambu
adhil adayaalam chinna thazhumbu

Aathadi Enna Udambu
Adi anganga pachcha narambu
iymponnaal senja odambu
adhil adayaalam chinna thazhumbu

natchathiram natchathiram
nattu vachcha kannu
un kannu, en kannu.

muthucharam muthucharam
kotti vachcha pallu
ye nillu, bathil sollu

kolaru thangam ellaam
un kannathil ottikichchaa
paalaru moththam vandhu
un kannathil kottikichaa

Aathadi Enna Udambu
Adi anganga pachcha narambu
iymponnaal senja odambu
adhil adayaalam chinna thazhumbu

tholumela thookkikittu
yezhu logam suththa
naan suththa, nee kaththa

mynaavukku thookkam vandhaa
maaman madi meththa
ponn meththa, poo meththa

sembatti vellarikkaa pola
siriyendi chella kanne.
nelakottai pooveduthu
naan jada pottu
paarpen ponne.

Aathadi Enna Udambu
Adi anganga pachcha narambu
iymponnaal senja odambu
adhil adayaalam chinna thazhumbu

enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the timeless charm of the song Aathadi Enna Udambu from the movie Sindhu Nathi Poo. This melodious duet, brought to life by the soulful voices of Shahul Hameed and Sujatha Mohan, captures the essence of love and admiration. With lyrics penned by the legendary Vairamuthu and music composed by Soundaryan, this track remains a cherished classic in Tamil cinema.
stanzaMeanings: Oh dear, what a body! Green veins and a golden-made physique, with small scars as marks.
Like the time of blooming flowers, like the golden bee's skin.
Oh dear, what a body! Green veins and a golden-made physique, with small scars as marks.
Stars, stars, the eyes placed in the country, your eyes, my eyes.
Pearls, pearls, the teeth set in rows, stop, answer.
Golden ornaments stuck to your cheeks, milk-like beauty gathered on your cheeks.
Now the skirt, later the upper cloth.
Oh dear, what a body! Green veins and a golden-made physique, with small scars as marks.
Carrying the upper cloth, I roam the seven worlds; I roam, you shout.
When the myna bird feels sleepy, it rests on uncle's lap, on the golden lap, on the flower lap.
Like the red cucumber, dear little one, my dear. I pluck the flower from the field and tie my braid, oh golden one.
Like the red mouth, like the small jasmine flower.
Oh dear, what a body! Green veins and a golden-made physique, with small scars as marks.
